title: Express.js Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Express.js commands and patterns for building web applications.

commands.scenario: Use when setting up a new Express.js application.

commands.command: const express = require('express'); const app = express();
commands.scenario: Use to start setting up your Express.js server.

commands.description: Start the Express server on a specified port.
commands.scenario: Use to start listening for incoming requests on port 3000.

commands.description: Define a GET route for the root URL.
commands.scenario: Use to handle GET requests to the root URL of your application.

commands.description: Enable JSON body parsing for incoming requests.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to parse JSON payloads in requests.

commands.description: Define a POST route to handle form submissions.
commands.scenario: Use to handle POST requests and access the request body.
Use to modularize routes in your Express application.

commands.description: Define a GET route on a router object.
commands.scenario: Use to handle GET requests within a modular router.

commands.description: Mount a router on a path in the app.
commands.scenario: Use to integrate a router into the main application with a specific path prefix.

commands.description: Set Pug as the view engine for the app.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to render views using the Pug template engine.

commands.description: Serve static files from a directory.
commands.scenario: Use to serve static assets like images, CSS files, and JavaScript files.

commands.command: app.use((err, req, res, next) => { res.status(500).send('Something broke!'); });
commands.scenario: Use to handle errors and send a response to the client.
